compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: STATUS (handoff): OPEN, NOT started. Plan not yet gate-hardened. KEY CONSTRAINT: AC 'On a real review the artifact lands on origin/tickets, linked relates_to all trailer-cited tickets' requires a LIVE reviewbot container + SSM PAT ā€” NOT exercisable from this worktree. Recommended approach: implement the code + a change-scoped code_review artifact TYPE (session_log-style: reducer-ignored, out of dep-graph, excluded from Jira sync, searchable), reuse plan_review.sidecar norm_id/material_fingerprint (no new algorithm), + the infra config (infra/compose/docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile.reviewbot: SSM PAT materialization + persistent tickets worktree + REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always) and the emit+trailer-link in src/rebar/review_bot/. Unit-test the artifact build + trailer resolution; mark the on-box PAT-push canary as an operational/deploy verification step (out of this ticket's automatable scope) so the completion-verifier judges the implementation, not the deploy. Also note the epic's own review flagged this as a T3-critical operational claim.

compiled_state.description:
# Code-review data capture: a change-scoped `code_review` artifact type + reviewbot emit

## Why
Code review persists NOTHING to the shared store today: the reviewbot only writes ephemeral
`/tmp` gate snapshots ā€” no `origin` remote, no push credential. So there is no durable record and
no corpus to calibrate the new impact model (`impact_code`, sibling `albite-lazy-barb`) from. We
want every review to leave a durable, searchable, ticket-linked artifact on the shared `tickets`
branch.

## Why a new ticket TYPE (not a sidecar-only record)
The artifact must be (a) SEARCHABLE by keyword and (b) LINKABLE (`relates_to`) to the work tickets
its change cites ā€” in the SAME store, so a corpus query can join reviews to tickets. A per-ticket
sidecar event cannot stand alone as a change-scoped record (a diff has no ticket), and a separate
store would not be searchable/linkable alongside work. So we mirror `session_log`: a first-class
ticket type that is out of the dependency graph, gate/lifecycle-exempt, excluded from Jira sync,
and hidden from default `list` but searchable ā€” exactly the properties a bulk artifact needs.

## What
Emit a change-scoped `code_review` artifact ticket keyed by `(gerrit change-id, revision)`,
carrying the code-review verdict (impact + tier-tagged sub-answers) as a sidecar on the artifact,
plus a diff-scoped `change_fingerprint` join key and per-finding `norm_id`s; and `relates_to`-link
it to EVERY rebar ticket cited by the change's commit `rebar-ticket:` trailers (many-to-one).

### A. The `code_review` ticket type (mirror `session_log` at EVERY guard site)
Add `code_review` to the type system and replicate `session_log`'s exemptions. Concrete sites
(verified present in the tree):
- Enum/source-of-truth: `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (`ticket_type` enum), then
  regenerate `src/rebar/types.py` via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` (do NOT hand-edit).
- Runtime validation: `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` `_TYPES` tuple (+ the hardcoded
  "Must be one of" error strings and `_USAGE`); add to `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES`.
- Store/graph exclusion: broaden the single reducer predicate at `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py`
  (`exclude_session_logs` KEEPS its name for back-compat; its predicate becomes `ticket_type not
  in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`, a new shared module constant). Callers that pass the flag INHERIT
  the broadened exclusion with NO edit: `graph/_graph.py`, `graph/_blockers.py`,
  `graph/_unblock.py` (never an open-child blocker), `graph/_ready.py`,
  `_engine_support/next_batch.py`. `graph/_hierarchy.py` is edited EXPLICITLY (NOT via the flag):
  its `_TYPE_TIER` gains `code_review: 0` and its blocking-link refusal reads the constant
  directly.
- Gate exemption: `_engine_support/gates.py` (check-ac / clarity / quality ā†’ gate-exempt pass);
  plan-review exemption axis `llm/plan_review/{orchestrator.py,workflow_ops.py,__init__.py,
  orchestrator.py,workflow_ops.py}` (the `("bug","session_log")` short-circuit ā†’ add `code_review`);
  and the plan-review CLAIM gate short-circuit in `_commands/gates.py`. (`__init__.py` / `det_floor.py` /
  `registry.py` are NOT edited ā€” they carry no such type short-circuit.)
  close gate `_commands/transition_close.py` (skip the billable verifier); the lifecycle
  guards in `_commands/transition.py` + `_commands/txn.py` (transition AND claim refusal) and the
  plan-review claim-gate exemption in `_commands/gates.py`.
- Jira exclusion: add `code_review` to `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES` in
  `_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py`; leave the two localā†’Jira type maps ā€”
  `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_TYPE` (`outbound_fields.py`) and `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA` (`reconcile_check.py`) ā€”
  untouched so any leak surfaces.
- Export: broaden the hardcoded `session_log` guard in `_io/export_ndjson.py` so default NDJSON
  exports exclude `code_review` too (opt in via `include_session_logs`).
- Visibility: `_engine_support/reads.py` ā€” hidden from default `list`, present in `search`/`show`
  (generalize the `exclude_session_logs=(ticket_type != ā€¦)` predicate).
- Lifecycle: like `session_log`, the artifact is NOT claimed/transitioned (the emit path CREATEs
  it directly; it is never dispatched).

### B. Join keys (what is reused vs what is genuinely new)
- `norm_id` IS reused verbatim: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:210` `norm_id(finding)`
  takes a finding dict (`finding`, `criteria`) ā€” code-review findings share that shape, so
  per-finding identity reuses it with NO new algorithm.
- `material_fingerprint` is NOT reusable: `plan_review/pass1.py:551` `material_fingerprint(ctx:
  PlanContext)` is ticket-scoped (ticket_id/description/file_impact/children) and a diff has no
  PlanContext. So we add ONE small NEW diff-scoped analogue `change_fingerprint(change_id,
  revision, changed_files, diff_text)` in the code-review sidecar module ā€” same construction as
  `material_fingerprint` (sha256 over a sorted-key JSON basis, 16-hex prefix), applied to the
  change instead of a ticket. This is the honest correction to the earlier "no new algorithm"
  claim: finding identity reuses `norm_id`; the change-scoped key is a small documented new
  function mirroring the existing one.

### C. Reviewbot emit + trailer linking (`src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py`)
In `review_and_vote`, AFTER the successful `store.record_vote` (voter.py ~496), where
`decision`, `commit_message`, `change_id`, `revision` are in scope:
0. COMPUTE `change_fingerprint(change_id, revision, changed_from_diff(diff_text), diff_text)` from
   the reviewed diff (the voter binds `diff_text` in the review block; it is "" on a review-error).
1. CREATE the `code_review` artifact ticket (`rebar.create_ticket("code_review", <title keyed by
   change-id+revision>, ā€¦)`); title carries the change-id + revision.
2. Emit the verdict sidecar onto the artifact by calling `code_review.sidecar.emit(...)` DIRECTLY. This
   requires EXTENDING the existing `code_review/sidecar.py` `emit(verdict, *, target_ticket,
   repo_root=None)` and `build_payload(verdict, *, target_ticket)` signatures with THREE NEW
   keyword params ā€” `change_id=""`, `revision=""`, `change_fp=""` (all defaulting empty so existing
   callers, e.g. the gate's `target_ticket` emit, are unaffected). `build_payload` is also extended
   to stamp per-finding `norm_id` (reused from plan-review) onto its blocking/advisory lists. The
   FULL verdict is threaded up from `adapter.code_review_decision` (a NEW `verdict` key ADDED to
   its return dict), so the voter does NOT re-invoke `produce_code_review_verdict`.
3. TRAILER RESOLUTION: `verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs(commit_message)` over the change's commit message (voter.py's `commit_message`, the
   fetched commit body; for a merge patchset that is the merge commit's message ā€” merge-parent
   trailer union is best-effort, out of scope here). For each ref, resolve via the store:
   - RESOLVABLE (a ticket with that alias/full/short id exists) ā†’ `rebar.link(artifact, ticket,
     "relates_to")`.
   - UNRESOLVED (well-formed ref, no such ticket) or FOREIGN (a Jira-key-shaped ref absent from
     the store) ā†’ log, non-fatal, continue.
   A decision that carries NO `verdict` key (e.g. the merge coverage-gap path via
   `_merge_coverage_gap_decision`, or a fail-closed review-error) emits NO artifact ā€” the voter reads
   `decision.get("verdict")` and returns early when it is absent/empty. All emit/link/push failures
   are caught and logged; they NEVER fail the review vote (the vote is already cast). Idempotency: the artifact is keyed by `(change-id, revision)` ā€” a re-review of
   the SAME revision reuses/updates the same artifact (look up by the change-key title before
   creating) so retries do not duplicate.
- LOG FORMAT (measurable): a single WARNING-level line per unresolved/foreign ref via the
  reviewbot logger ā€” `logger.warning("code_review artifact %s: unresolved rebar-ticket trailer
  %r (change %s/%s)", artifact_id, ref, change_id, revision)` ā€” and one INFO summary line
  `logger.info("code_review artifact %s: linked %d/%d trailer refs", artifact_id, linked, total)`.

### D. Infra (config in-repo; the on-box push is a DEPLOY-STEP canary, un-verifiable in-session)
- SSM slot: add the leaf `reviewbot-tickets-pat` to `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params`
  (a SecureString the operator populates with the RebarBotNava contents:write PAT ā€” a DEPLOY
  prerequisite).
- Materialization: add the `reviewbot-tickets-pat ā†’ REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` leafā†’env mapping to
  `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` (writes `infra/compose/.env`, 0600).
- Container: `infra/compose/{docker-compose.yml,Dockerfile.reviewbot}` ā€” set
  `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` AND `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=/var/gerrit/site/reviewbot-tickets` (so rebar's
  ambient `config.tracker_dir` ā€” read by the reviewbot's `repo_root=None` create/link/emit calls ā€”
  resolves to the persistent worktree, NOT the container `/app` cwd); on startup materialize that
  PERSISTENT tickets worktree once (clone of the `tickets` branch onto the mounted volume) and
  configure a URL-SCOPED (origin only, NOT global) git credential
  helper that echoes `username=x-access-token` / `password=$REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` so the token
  never lands on disk or in a remote URL. Concurrency: voter.py holds a per-(change_id, revision) `asyncio.Lock`, so two reviews of the SAME
  revision cannot race the idempotent lookup-then-create; DIFFERENT changes write DISTINCT artifact
  tickets (distinct event files) into the append-only, auto-committing store, so they need no extra
  lock either.
- The actual on-box PAT push is a DEPLOY-STEP CANARY (requires SSM + the live container) and is
  therefore NOT verifiable in this session; the completion-verifier judges the IMPLEMENTATION
  (the artifact-build + trailer-resolution code + the infra config), not the live push.

## Scope / files
- Lifecycle/export guards: `src/rebar/_commands/{transition.py,txn.py,transition_close.py,gates.py}`,
  `src/rebar/_io/export_ndjson.py` (mirror the session_log guards).
- Type: `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json`, `src/rebar/types.py` (regen),
  `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py`, `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py`, `src/rebar/graph/*.py`,
  `src/rebar/_engine_support/{gates.py,next_batch.py,reads.py}`,
  `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/{orchestrator.py,workflow_ops.py,__init__.py,det_floor.py,registry.py}`,
  `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`, `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py`.
- Emit + keys: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` (`change_fingerprint`, augment payload),
  `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` (thread the full `verdict` up on `code_review_decision`'s
  return), `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py` (create artifact + trailer-link).
- Infra: `infra/compose/{docker-compose.yml,Dockerfile.reviewbot}`,
  `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`, `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`.
- Tests: `tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `code_review` is a valid ticket type (in `common.schema.json` + regenerated `types.py` +
      `composer._TYPES`) and is mirror-exempt like `session_log` at every guard site: excluded from
      the reducer/dependency-graph/`ready`/`next_batch`, gate-exempt (check-ac/clarity/quality +
      plan-review + close), excluded from Jira sync (`EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES`, both Jira maps
      untouched), and hidden from default `list` but returned by `search`. Proving command:
      `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py -v`.
- [ ] `change_fingerprint(change_id, revision, changed_files, diff_text)` exists in the code-review
      sidecar module and returns a stable 16-hex fingerprint (same construction as
      `material_fingerprint`); per-finding identity reuses `plan_review.sidecar.norm_id` verbatim.
      Unit-tested (stability + sensitivity to each input). Proving command:
      `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py -v`.
- [ ] The reviewbot emit path builds a `code_review` artifact payload for a review verdict and
      resolves `rebar-ticket:` trailers into `relates_to` links: RESOLVABLE refs are linked;
      UNRESOLVED/FOREIGN refs are logged at WARNING (with the specified format) and are NON-FATAL;
      the artifact is idempotent per `(change-id, revision)`. Unit-tested with a fake store
      (resolvable ref ā†’ link; unresolved ref ā†’ logged, not raised). Proving command:
      `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py -v`.
- [ ] Infra config lands in-repo: `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` + a persistent tickets-worktree volume
      in `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`, a PER-URL (not global) git credential helper +
      one-time tickets-worktree materialization in `infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot`, the
      `reviewbot-tickets-pat ā†’ REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` mapping in `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`,
      and the `/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat` SSM slot in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`. Proving
      command (presence of all four in-repo edits): `grep -q 'REBAR_SYNC_PUSH' infra/compose/docker-compose.yml
      && grep -q 'REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT' infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh && grep -q 'reviewbot-tickets-pat'
      infra/terraform/ssm.tf && grep -qE 'credential\.[^ ]+\.helper|x-access-token' infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot`. The live
      on-box PAT push is a DEPLOY-STEP canary (needs SSM + a running container) and is explicitly
      OUT of in-session verification ā€” the completion-verifier judges the IMPLEMENTATION, not the push.
- [ ] No retention cap / prune path is added for the `code_review` artifact set (deliberate ā€” it
      IS the calibration corpus). Observable: no code prunes/caps `code_review` tickets. Proving
      command: `! grep -rniE 'prune|retention|cap|evict' src/rebar/ --include='*.py' | grep -i code_review`
      (no prune/retention/cap logic mentions code_review anywhere under src/rebar).

## Implementation precision (grounds the acceptance criteria)
- `reads.py` list-visibility uses EXACTLY `exclude_session_logs=(ticket_type not in
  _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES)` (the shared `("session_log","code_review")` set) so default `list`
  hides BOTH artifact types while `list --type=code_review` shows them; the downstream
  `apply_ticket_filters(type_filter=...)` narrows to the exact type.
- The `reducer/_api.py` `exclude_session_logs` boolean KEEPS its name (back-compat ā€” NO caller
  migration) but its predicate broadens to `ticket_type not in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`, so every
  existing `exclude_session_logs=True` caller (ready/next_batch/deps/validate/list) transparently
  excludes `code_review` too. The new set is a single module constant.
- Idempotency lookup uses `rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")` and matches on the
  `title` field == `f"code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}"`; on any lookup failure the fallback
  is to CREATE a fresh artifact (accepting a rare duplicate over dropping the record).
- The git credential helper is URL-SCOPED via the `credential.<tickets-url>.helper` config key
  (set by the Dockerfile entrypoint before the worktree clone), echoing `username=x-access-token` /
  `password=$REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` so the token never lands on disk or in a remote URL. rebar
  resolves its store via `config.tracker_dir` ā€” which reads `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` (canonical env) ā€”
  so the compose `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=/var/gerrit/site/reviewbot-tickets` points the ambient store at
  the persistent worktree (the emit runs OUTSIDE the temp code-clone `with` block, so it MUST NOT
  use that deleted tmpdir ā€” it passes `repo_root=None` and relies on this env). Both the helper
  wiring and store resolution are exercised only by the DEPLOY-STEP canary (un-verifiable in-session).
compiled_state.title: Code-review data capture to the rebar ticket store (change-scoped, PAT-credentialed)
